_doc: Sprint 14 / S14.B — recorded happy-path GET /api/admin/reviews/rev-fixture-001. Three findings; one carries a suggestion. Used by frontend MSW handlers + e2e Playwright stubs against the locked ReviewDetailV1 envelope.

findings.title: Endpoint accepts cross-tenant installation_id without filter
findings.body: GET /v2/users/{user_id} resolves the user without joining on installation_id. A reader in tenant A can read tenant B's user record by guessing a UUID. Add the tenancy hook decorator and a join filter.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the resolver in @tenancy_required and filter the SELECT on installation_id from the session.

findings.title: Audit emit fires before write commits
findings.body: audit_emit() is called inside the request handler but BEFORE the SQLAlchemy session commits. If the commit fails the audit row already says 'updated' — drift between audit log and reality. Move the emit into an after-commit hook.

findings.title: Cross-tenant test uses the same installation_id for both tenants
findings.body: The fixture seeds installation_id=tenant_A in both setUp arms. The cross-tenant denial assertion therefore never exercises the production code path that compares the cookie installation_id to the row's. Seed two distinct UUIDs.
